meaning: n.火把,启发之物vi.像火炬一样燃烧vt.用火炬点燃
examples: One of the men shone a torch in his face.
Len studied it a moment in the beam of his torch.
They struggled to keep the torch of idealism and hope alive.
The large-scale event will include musical performances, a fire ceremony and torch run.
The carrier contacts I have talked to have little faith in the new Torch devices.
